features.description: Neon schema migrations: port the 8 Supabase SQL migrations to db/migrations/ (drop pg_cron and RLS/anon/authenticated constructs; keep memories table with vector(1536) + HNSW index, system_config, and the three SQL functions), add a psql-based migration runner, and write the Neon provisioning runbook for Ovidiu. ACCEPTANCE: runner applies all migrations to an empty Neon database idempotently (second run is a no-op, exit 0); schema assertion script against a Neon branch confirms expected objects present and no pg_cron/RLS/anon/authenticated grants.
features.coverage: not measurable (bash scripts; vitest v8 coverage does not apply). Behavioral test covers both scripts end-to-end: 16 checks incl. idempotency, schema presence, dropped-construct absence, and both failure paths.
features.notes: Acceptance verified against the real Neon database (ep-curly-glade-ab0ouns8, aws-eu-west-2, PG 18) on 2026-07-03: run 1 = 5 applied, run 2 = no-op exit 0, assert-schema.sh all 16 checks OK. 8 source migrations became 5: RLS (004) and pg_cron/process_pending_memories (005) dropped per AD-3/AD-7, 008 constraint folded into the new 002 (fresh start, identical end state).
approaches_tried: TDD via bash behavioral test against a local pgvector/pg17 Docker container, then acceptance re-run against the provisioned Neon DB. psql runner records each migration in schema_migrations inside the same --single-transaction as the migration file. Worked first pass.

features.description: MCP server DB layer on Neon: replace the supabase-js client factory (mcp-server/src/db/client.ts) with @neondatabase/serverless, rewrite all queries.ts functions to plain SQL, remove @supabase/supabase-js from mcp-server/package.json, add @vitest/coverage-v8 so coverage is measurable. ACCEPTANCE: existing Vitest suite green + new integration round-trip test against a Neon branch (capture_memory -> search_brain finds it -> list_recent lists it -> get_stats counts it); coverage >= 95% on touched code.
features.coverage: client.ts 100%, queries.ts 100% lines / 97.7% branch (>=95% gate met). Disclosed exception: transport/sse.ts at 0% — 3 lines touched as forced compile-fix (health endpoint used getSupabaseClient); file has no test harness and is deleted by F006 per AD-4.
features.notes: Acceptance verified 2026-07-03: full suite 77/77 green incl. round-trip against Neon branch 'test' (br-morning-morning-ab8igqsz): capture -> search (similarity > 0.99) -> list_recent -> get_stats, plus AD-6 degradation path (embedding failure -> pending row, NULL embedding). supabase-js removed from mcp-server/package.json.
scope_expansions: mcp-server/src/transport/sse.ts: health endpoint called getSupabaseClient directly; removing supabase-js forced a 3-line rewrite to plain SQL via getDb. File is removed entirely by F006 (AD-4).
approaches_tried: TDD: 25 unit tests mocking the neon tagged-template driver (red on supabase impl), then rewrite. Retry increments became single atomic UPDATE ... CASE statements (old code was racy read-then-update). Integration test env-gated on NEON_TEST_DATABASE_URL, LLM services stubbed, DB real. Worked with one type fix: ReturnType<typeof neon> is a config-dependent union; pin NeonQueryFunction<false, false>.

features.description: Shared Worker DB module at workers/shared/: Neon driver wrapper, env handling, and the common SQL used by the Workers. ACCEPTANCE: module builds standalone; unit tests cover connection creation, query helpers, and a ::vector cast round-trip (insert an embedding array, read it back identically).
features.coverage: 100% statements/branch/functions/lines on index.ts (types.ts is declaration-only, no runtime code).
features.notes: Acceptance verified 2026-07-03: standalone build green (own package.json/tsconfig, not a root workspace — 'builds standalone' taken literally, zero root coupling); 17/17 tests incl. ::vector round-trip against Neon branch 'test' (1536-dim embedding inserted and read back identically). API takes the sql handle as a parameter (createDb(url)) because Workers get config from env bindings, not process.env. TDD slip: tests and implementation written in the same batch, red phase not observed for this feature.
approaches_tried: Mirrored the F002 queries.ts idiom (sanitized errors, toIso/parseVector normalization, atomic retry increments) with the connection passed explicitly. Green first run.

features.description: Capture Worker: port supabase/functions/capture to a Cloudflare Worker fetch handler with JWT HS256 + HMAC auth preserved verbatim, Neon insert with explicit ::vector cast, and AD-6 degradation (on embedding/metadata failure the memory is stored with embedding_status 'pending'/'failed'). ACCEPTANCE: deployed Worker returns 201 with memory id for validly signed requests (JWT and HMAC paths both tested), 401 for invalid/missing auth, 400 for invalid payload; on forced embedding failure the row lands with embedding_status='pending'.
features.test_file: workers/capture/src/index.test.ts (+auth, rate-limit, embedding, metadata test files; 73 tests)
features.notes: Coverage 100% stmts/lines/fns, 97.97% branches. Deployment verification (deployed-Worker acceptance clause) deferred to Ovidiu per approved plan.
approaches_tried: Single pass, 0 corrections. Web Crypto for JWT HS256 + HMAC (no nodejs_compat). Deviations per spec: 201 not source 200; no wildcard CORS. @cloudflare/workers-types must pin ^4.x (wrangler@4 peer dep ERESOLVEs on ^5.x). Local CaptureMetadata extends shared MemoryMetadata to add sentiment without touching workers/shared. Discovered live wildcard-CORS regression in supabase/functions/capture/index.ts (reintroduced by 83b19ec after 6b72bf6 fixed it) — Worker does not carry it forward.

features.description: Retry Worker: port supabase/functions/retry-worker to a Cloudflare Worker scheduled() handler on a '* * * * *' Cron Trigger, no public HTTP route; retry eligibility comes solely from the get_retry_eligible_memories() SQL function (the PostgREST .or() fallback is not ported, per AD-7). ACCEPTANCE: cron-triggered run processes a seeded pending memory to complete within one scheduled invocation; HTTP requests to the Worker return 404/405; overlapping runs do not double-process (status transitions + batch limit).
features.test_file: workers/retry/src/retry-batch.test.ts (+5 unit test files incl. metadata-service.test.ts 17 tests; +1 integration env-gated on NEON_TEST_DATABASE_URL)
features.notes: Review F2 accepted: no row locking on overlapping cron runs, matches source; worst case duplicate LLM spend, not corruption. Deployment verification (cron trigger live) deferred to Ovidiu.
approaches_tried: Faithful port of supabase/functions/retry-worker: scheduled() -> runRetryBatch via ctx.waitUntil; fetch() 404 unconditionally (no public route). Eligibility solely via workers/shared getRetryEligibleMemories (AD-7 — no PostgREST fallback ported). Terminal metadata_status=failed at 10 retries inherited from workers/shared atomic UPDATE...CASE (F003), not reimplemented. Per-record embedding+metadata retried in parallel like the Deno source. 39 unit tests green first pass, 100% coverage.

Review F1 fix (e89e965, TDD 5-red-first): validateMetadata aligned to capture semantics — 9-type list, coerce-to-unknown (no throw), 50-item array caps, sentiment passthrough; non-object JSON still a retryable throw. Suite 44 passing, coverage held at 100%.

features.description: Remote MCP Worker: stateless Streamable HTTP via the Cloudflare Agents SDK createMcpHandler(), exposing the four existing tools (search_brain, list_recent, get_stats, capture_memory); JWT issuance endpoint (/auth/token) ported; Express SSE host (mcp-server/src/transport/sse.ts) removed. ACCEPTANCE: an MCP client connects over Streamable HTTP to the Worker URL with a JWT from /auth/token and successfully invokes all four tools; unauthenticated connection is rejected.
features.notes: Coverage 98.8% stmts/lines (env.ts declaration-only at 0%, jwt.ts 91% on narrow catch branches). Protocol-level Streamable HTTP handshake still mock-tested only — real MCP client connect is the deployment verification step reserved for Ovidiu.
scope_expansions: mcp-server/src/index.ts + mcp-server/package.json: sse.ts deletion orphans the startSSETransport import and --sse-only branch; express dep dropped only if grep confirms unused (same expansion F002 disclosed)
approaches_tried: Plan-approved WebCrypto JWT (no nodejs_compat for own code) + jsonwebtoken cross-impl compatibility test. wrangler deploy --dry-run (disclosed deviation, no credentials) caught two bundling bugs unit tests could not: Agents SDK itself requires nodejs_compat (docs-confirmed), and its client-side `await import("ai")` needs a wrangler [alias] no-op stub since only createMcpHandler is used. server.test.ts spies on McpServer.prototype.registerTool instead of SDK introspection. SSE host + orphaned auth/middleware.ts + auth/jwt.ts removed from mcp-server (69/71 suite unaffected).

features.description: CLI setup rewrite (personal-use scope; 60-min non-coder test dropped per 2026-07-03 decision): Neon connection step, migration-runner step, wrangler deploy/secrets steps; validate.ts and status.ts off supabase-js; Supabase-specific steps removed. ACCEPTANCE: setup completes end-to-end on a machine with a Neon connection string and authenticated wrangler (writes .env, runs migrations, deploys the three Workers, sets secrets); status command reports all components healthy with no supabase-js dependency.
features.notes: Coverage on touched files: 95.7-100% stmts (untouched pre-existing ui/openai/anthropic/secrets steps remain untested, out of gate). E2E acceptance clause (real Neon string + authenticated wrangler) = Ovidiu deployment-verification session; combines with the pending Phase-2 Worker deploy verification.
approaches_tried: TDD red-first per module (module-not-found red for new steps, behavioral red for rewires). 8-step flow kept the SetupStep contract intact so only steps changed, not the runner. Corrected mid-plan: secrets.ts stays (it GENERATES the HMAC/JWT/client secrets; wrangler only uploads them). Migrations shell out to scripts/migrate.sh with the -pooler stripped (direct endpoint gotcha). wrangler step deploys 3 workers, uploads only present secrets via stdin, persists workers.dev URLs to .env for the status command. State file v2 normalizes old v1 files (edgeFunctionsDeployed dropped, workersDeployed added).

features.description: Cutover + decommission: fresh Neon data (Supabase data explicitly not preserved, decided 2026-07-03), reconfigure Claude Desktop stdio env, point webhook callers at the capture Worker custom domain (workers.dev interim fallback), verify end-to-end, decommission Supabase project lxwtqegyhrfixnfctkne. Downtime unconstrained. ACCEPTANCE: runbook executed — Claude Desktop stdio works against Neon; capture Worker reachable on its domain; retry cron observed processing; Supabase decommissioned only after all other criteria observed green (rollback bound).
features.test_file: live acceptance (docs/cutover-runbook.md); no unit tests — operational feature
features.notes: Supabase lxwtqegyhrfixnfctkne PAUSED not deleted — rollback bound per acceptance; permanent delete is a later one-click. Custom domain not configured; workers.dev interim per spec.
approaches_tried: Executed autonomously under blanket go-ahead: wrangler OAuth (one browser click), 3 Workers deployed + secrets shell-piped from .env (values never in transcript), live acceptance (capture 201 JWT + sha256=HMAC, 401/400/replay, MCP initialize/tools-list/all-4-tools over Streamable HTTP, stdio handshake vs Neon), Claude Desktop reconfigured, retry cron observed repairing seeded pending+degraded rows, Supabase project paused (rollback-bound) only after all green. Two live bugs found+fixed: Anthropic account has NO API credits (provider switched to openai fleet-wide), retry selectCaller never falls back to OPENAI_API_KEY for metadata (operational fix: OPENAI_METADATA_API_KEY secret set on retry; code divergence folds into F010).

features.description: Docs + spec amendment PR: amend open-brain-spec.md §1.3 (drop 60-minute non-coder setup test; personal-use), §5 Architecture and §8 Deployment for the Neon + Cloudflare stack; update README; .env.example gains DATABASE_URL and the missing OPENAI_METADATA_API_KEY, loses all SUPABASE_* vars; replace docs/deploy-to-supabase.md. ACCEPTANCE: spec PR merged or approved by Ovidiu; .env.example matches the new stack with no SUPABASE_* vars; README quickstart matches the new stack.
features.notes: ../open-brain-spec.md (loose parent-dir file) is now a stale duplicate — superseded by docs/open-brain-spec.md; flagged to Ovidiu.
approaches_tried: Spec brought INTO the repo (docs/open-brain-spec.md v1.1.0) making the Prime Rule real for the first time; amendment delegated to a context-inheriting fork (35 clusters, 91 stack mentions; ADR bodies preserved under amendment banners). Three spec-vs-reality corrections grounded in deployed schema: metadata_status failed terminal state, 9-type enum, capture 201.

features.description: Consolidate the embedding/metadata service copies and MCP db queries (duplicated across workers/capture, workers/retry, workers/mcp per the accepted Phase-2 structural duplication) into workers/shared, unifying metadata validation semantics (one VALID_TYPES list, coerce-to-unknown, array caps, sentiment passthrough) so the three-way divergence found in review (F1/F3, 2026-07-04) cannot recur. ACCEPTANCE: single implementation in workers/shared consumed by all three Workers; all Worker suites green; no behavior change at the HTTP/tool contract level.
approaches_tried: TDD: shared services.test.ts written red-first incl. an explicit regression test for the retry key-fallback bug (provider=openai + only OPENAI_API_KEY must call OpenAI). Unified semantics: 9-type coerce-to-unknown validation, 50-item caps, sentiment passthrough, one prompt, provider chain honoring requested provider with cross-provider missing-key fallback. Shared extractMetadata/fetchEmbedding THROW; degrading consumers (capture/mcp) wrap in allSettled, retry counts throws — per-consumer error contracts preserved. mcp reject-on-invalid-type validator and 7-type prompt superseded (deliberate, per feature spec). MCP read queries moved to shared/queries.ts with db-util extraction. 13 duplicated files deleted. Bundle dry-runs clean; redeployed; live smoke green (201 + semantic search hit).
features.test_file: workers/shared/src/services.test.ts (23) + queries.test.ts; suites: shared 47, capture 49, retry 19, mcp 68
features.notes: mcp behavior change within spec: off-list metadata type now stored as unknown/ready instead of degraded (unification mandate). types.ts declaration-only at 0% (established exception).

features.description: Delete capability (backlog BI-001): delete_memory MCP tool on both the stdio server and the MCP Worker. Hard delete by exact id only (no bulk, no delete-by-query); not-found returns an explicit error; no HTTP delete endpoint on the capture Worker. ACCEPTANCE: spec amended via merged PR (tool contract + data retention); deleteMemory in workers/shared and mcp-server db layers with tests; tool registered and tested in both MCP hosts; live proof on the deployed Worker (capture -> delete -> search confirms gone); all suites green, coverage >=95% on touched code.
approaches_tried: Spec-first (v1.2.0, FR-DEL-01..04) then TDD red-first in both db layers and both hosts. One SQL statement (DELETE..RETURNING id) implemented twice by design (workers/shared + standalone mcp-server). Worker registration order must match TOOL_NAMES (server.test asserts order). Live proof on deployed Worker: capture -> delete {deleted:true} -> second delete NOT_FOUND -> search confirms gone; stdio host verified listing 5 tools.
features.test_file: workers/shared queries.test.ts (+3), mcp-server db/queries.test.ts (+3) + tools/delete-memory.test.ts, workers/mcp tools/delete-memory.test.ts + server.test.ts envelope tests
features.notes: No HTTP delete on capture Worker per FR-DEL-04. stdio needs no deploy (local dist rebuilt).
